candidates.title: LIBERO-Para: A Diagnostic Benchmark and Metrics for Paraphrase Robustness in VLA Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models achieve strong performance in robotic manipulation by leveraging pre-trained vision-language backbones. candidates.title: TAG: Target-Agnostic Guidance for Stable Object-Centric Inference in Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision--Language--Action (VLA) policies have shown strong progress in mapping language instructions and visual observations to robotic actions, yet their reliability degrades in cluttered scenes with distractors. candidates.title: UniDriveVLA: Unifying Understanding, Perception, and Action Planning for Autonomous Driving

candidates.title: Uni-World VLA: Interleaved World Modeling and Planning for Autonomous Driving
candidates.abstract_snippet: Autonomous driving requires reasoning about how the environment evolves and planning actions accordingly. candidates.title: DFM-VLA: Iterative Action Refinement for Robot Manipulation via Discrete Flow Matching

candidates.title: SABER: A Stealthy Agentic Black-Box Attack Framework for Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-language-action (VLA) models enable robots to follow natural-language instructions grounded in visual observations, but the instruction channel also introduces a critical vulnerability: small textual perturbations can alter downstream robot behavior. candidates.title: DriveDreamer-Policy: A Geometry-Grounded World-Action Model for Unified Generation and Planning
candidates.abstract_snippet: Recently, world-action models (WAM) have emerged to bridge vision-language-action (VLA) models and world models, unifying their reasoning and instruction-following capabilities and spatio-temporal world modeling. candidates.title: Boosting Vision-Language-Action Finetuning with Feasible Action Neighborhood Prior
candidates.abstract_snippet: In real-world robotic manipulation, states typically admit a neighborhood of near-equivalent actions. candidates.abstract_snippet: Deploying learned robot manipulation policies in industrial settings requires rigorous pre-deployment validation, yet exhaustive testing across high-dimensional parameter spaces is intractable. candidates.title: UAV-Track VLA: Embodied Aerial Tracking via Vision-Language-Action Models

candidates.title: CaP-X: A Framework for Benchmarking and Improving Coding Agents for Robot Manipulation
candidates.abstract_snippet: "Code-as-Policy" considers how executable code can complement data-intensive Vision-Language-Action (VLA) methods, yet their effectiveness as autonomous controllers for embodied manipulation remains underexplored. candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models improve action generation by conditioning policies on rich vision-language information. candidates.title: DiffusionAnything: End-to-End In-context Diffusion Learning for Unified Navigation and Pre-Grasp Motion

candidates.title: LILAC: Language-Conditioned Object-Centric Optical Flow for Open-Loop Trajectory Generation
candidates.abstract_snippet: We address language-conditioned robotic manipulation using flow-based trajectory generation, which enables training on human and web videos of object manipulation and requires only minimal embodiment-specific data. candidates.title: SG-VLA: Learning Spatially-Grounded Vision-Language-Action Models for Mobile Manipulation

candidates.title: Causal Scene Narration with Runtime Safety Supervision for Vision-Language-Action Driving

candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-language models (VLMs) have achieved impressive results on single-view vision tasks, but lack the multi-view spatial reasoning capabilities essential for embodied AI systems to understand 3D environments and manipulate objects across different viewpoints. candidates.title: Beyond Attention Magnitude: Leveraging Inter-layer Rank Consistency for Efficient Vision-Language-Action Models

candidates.title: Gaze-Regularized Vision-Language-Action Models for Robotic Manipulation

candidates.title: AffordTissue: Dense Affordance Prediction for Tool-Action Specific Tissue Interaction

candidates.title: Vega: Learning to Drive with Natural Language Instructions

candidates.title: CoMaTrack: Competitive Multi-Agent Game-Theoretic Tracking with Vision-Language-Action Models

candidates.title: Fast-dVLA: Accelerating Discrete Diffusion VLA to Real-Time Performance

candidates.title: AI Token Futures Market: Commoditization of Compute and Derivatives Contract Design
candidates.abstract_snippet: As large language models (LLMs) and vision-language-action models (VLAs) become widely deployed, the tokens consumed by AI inference are evolving into a new type of commodity. candidates.title: CLaD: Planning with Grounded Foresight via Cross-Modal Latent Dynamics
candidates.abstract_snippet: Robotic manipulation involves kinematic and semantic transitions that are inherently coupled via underlying actions.
